annotations.label: Median of a set

anchor.snippet: the value separating the higher half from the lower half

annotations.note: Mathlib has no statistical median; the only `median` declaration is the geometric median of a simplex in Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/AffineSpace/Simplex/Centroid.lean, which is unrelated.

annotations.label: Median as 2-quantile

anchor.snippet: the value that partitions a set into two equal parts

annotations.note: No quantile or 2-quantile/median machinery exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median of a finite list

anchor.snippet: The median of a finite list of numbers is the

annotations.note: No median of a finite list/multiset is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median of odd-sized set

anchor.snippet: the middle one is selected

annotations.note: No notion of the middle order statistic / median for finite sets exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Seven-number median is 6

anchor.snippet: which is the fourth value

annotations.note: No median definition exists, so this worked example cannot be expressed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median of even-sized set

anchor.snippet: the median is usually defined to be the arithmetic mean of the two middle values

annotations.note: No median (averaging the two middle values) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Eight-number median is 4.5

anchor.snippet: has a median value of 4.5

annotations.note: No median definition exists, so this example is not formalizable in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General median formula

anchor.snippet: the median can be defined as follows

annotations.note: The general (sorted-data) median formula is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Formal median of a population

anchor.snippet: a median of a population is any value such that at least half of the population is less than or equal to the proposed median

annotations.note: No population/measure median (CDF ≥ 1/2 condition) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median well-defined for ordered data

anchor.snippet: The median is well-defined for any ordered (one-dimensional) data

annotations.note: Without a median definition there is no well-definedness statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric median dimensions

anchor.snippet: is defined in any number of dimensions

annotations.note: The geometric (spatial) median is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median as quartile/decile/percentile

anchor.snippet: it is the 2nd quartile

annotations.note: No quartile/decile/percentile concepts exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multiset median is 2

anchor.snippet: The median is 2 in this case

annotations.note: No multiset median is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median of a random variable

anchor.snippet: A median of a real-valued random variable

annotations.note: Mathlib defines expectation/variance but has no median of a random variable.

anchors.snippet: A median of a real-valued random variable

annotations.label: Existence and non-uniqueness

anchor.snippet: always exists, but needs not be uniquely determined

annotations.note: No median existence/uniqueness results exist since the median is undefined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: always exists, but needs not be uniquely determined

annotations.label: At least one median exists

anchor.snippet: has at least one median, but in pathological cases there may be more than one median

annotations.note: No median existence theorem exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric unimodal median equals mode

anchor.snippet: The median of a symmetric unimodal distribution coincides with the mode

annotations.note: Neither median nor mode of a distribution is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric distribution median equals mean

anchor.snippet: also takes the value μ

annotations.note: Mathlib has expectation but no median, so this median=mean fact is absent.

annotations.label: Median of normal distribution

anchor.snippet: The median of a normal distribution with mean μ and variance

annotations.note: The Gaussian distribution `gaussianReal` is formalized, but its median (=μ) is not stated since Mathlib lacks a median definition.

annotations.label: Median of uniform distribution

anchor.snippet: The median of a uniform distribution in the interval

annotations.note: Uniform distributions exist via `IsUniform`, but the median (midpoint) is not formalized as Mathlib has no median.

annotations.label: Median of Cauchy distribution

anchor.snippet: The median of a Cauchy distribution with location parameter

annotations.note: The Cauchy distribution `cauchyMeasure` is formalized, but its median (=location parameter) is not stated.

annotations.label: Median of power law distribution

anchor.snippet: The median of a power law distribution

annotations.note: The Pareto (power-law) distribution `paretoMeasure` exists, but its median is not computed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median of exponential distribution

anchor.snippet: The median of an exponential distribution with rate parameter

annotations.note: The exponential distribution `expMeasure` is formalized, but its median (ln2/λ) is not stated.

annotations.label: Median of Weibull distribution

anchor.snippet: The median of a Weibull distribution with shape parameter

annotations.note: Neither the Weibull distribution nor a median concept exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median minimizes mean absolute error

anchor.snippet: m is a median of X if and only if m is a minimizer of the mean absolute error

annotations.note: The L1-optimality characterization of the median is absent (no median in Mathlib).

anchors.snippet: m is a median of X if and only if m is a minimizer of the mean absolute error

annotations.label: k-medians clustering use

anchor.snippet: useful in statistical data-analysis

annotations.note: Clustering algorithms such as k-medians are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mean-median distance bound

anchor.snippet: is bounded by one standard deviation

annotations.note: The |mean−median| ≤ σ inequality is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Multivariate mean-median inequality

anchor.snippet: a multivariate version of the inequality

annotations.note: No multivariate mean–median inequality exists in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: a multivariate version of the inequality

annotations.label: Uniqueness of spatial median

anchor.snippet: The spatial median is unique when the data-set's dimension is two or more

annotations.note: The spatial/geometric median and its uniqueness are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sharper unimodal mean-median bound

anchor.snippet: one can achieve a sharper bound on the distance between the median and the mean

annotations.note: No unimodal mean–median bound is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median-mode relation (unimodal)

anchor.snippet: A similar relation holds between the median and the mode

annotations.note: Neither median nor mode is defined for distributions in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Positive skew heuristic

anchor.snippet: positively skewed distributions have mean > median

annotations.note: Skewness and the mean>median heuristic are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decreasing density: median < mean

anchor.snippet: the median is less than the mean

annotations.note: This density-monotonicity result on median vs mean is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jensen's inequality

anchor.snippet: Jensen's inequality states that for any random variable

annotations.note: The classic Jensen inequality f(E[X]) ≤ E[f(X)] for convex f under a probability measure is exactly `ConvexOn.map_integral_le` (finite-sum form: `ConvexOn.map_sum_le`).

annotations.label: C function

anchor.snippet: We say a function f : R → R is a C function

annotations.note: The article-specific 'C function' class (Merkle) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex functions are C functions

anchor.snippet: Every convex function is a C function

annotations.note: The 'C function' notion is absent, so this implication is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: Every convex function is a C function

annotations.label: Jensen's inequality for medians

anchor.snippet: If f is a C function, then

annotations.note: The median version of Jensen's inequality is not in Mathlib (no median defined).

annotations.label: Linear-time selection of median

anchor.section: Efficient computation of the sample median
anchor.snippet: selection algorithms can compute the k th-smallest of n items

annotations.note: Linear-time selection algorithms and their complexity are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median of three rule

anchor.section: Efficient computation of the sample median
anchor.snippet: the median of three rule, which estimates the median as the median of a three-element subsample

annotations.note: The median-of-three heuristic is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tukey's ninther

anchor.section: Efficient computation of the sample median
anchor.snippet: Tukey 's ninther , which is the median of three rule applied with limited recursion

annotations.note: Tukey's ninther is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Remedian

anchor.section: Efficient computation of the sample median
anchor.snippet: The remedian is an estimator for the median that requires linear time but sub-linear memory

annotations.note: The remedian estimator is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Asymptotic normality of sample median

anchor.snippet: is asymptotically normal with mean

annotations.note: Mathlib has a CLT for sums but no asymptotic normality result for the sample median.

annotations.label: Variance of median for normal samples

anchor.snippet: for large samples the variance of the median equals

annotations.note: The asymptotic variance of the sample median is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median density is symmetric beta

anchor.snippet: the density function of the median is a symmetric beta distribution

annotations.note: The Beta distribution `betaMeasure` is formalized, but the order-statistic result that the sample median's density is a symmetric Beta is not.

annotations.label: Consistency of delete-k estimator

anchor.section: Estimation of variance from sample data
anchor.snippet: has been shown to be asymptotically consistent

annotations.note: The delete-k variance estimator and its consistency are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Efficiency of sample median (normal)

anchor.snippet: the efficiency for large N is

annotations.note: Statistical efficiency of the sample median is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hodges–Lehmann estimator

anchor.snippet: the Hodges–Lehmann estimator is a robust and highly efficient estimator of the population median

annotations.note: The Hodges–Lehmann estimator is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pareto interpolation

anchor.snippet: Pareto interpolation is an application of this

annotations.note: Pareto interpolation as an estimation method is not in Mathlib (only the Pareto distribution and unrelated polynomial interpolation exist).

annotations.label: Multivariate median agrees in 1D

anchor.snippet: each such multivariate median agrees with the univariate median when the dimension is exactly one

annotations.note: No multivariate or univariate median is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Marginal median

anchor.snippet: the vector whose components are univariate medians

annotations.note: The marginal median is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric median

anchor.snippet: point minimizing the sum of distances to the sample points

annotations.note: The geometric median (minimizer of summed distances) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric median equivariance

anchor.snippet: the geometric median is equivariant with respect to Euclidean similarity transformations

annotations.note: No geometric median, hence no equivariance result, exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median in all directions

anchor.snippet: their common location may be termed the

annotations.note: The 'median in all directions' concept is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coincides with geometric median

anchor.snippet: the median in all directions coincides with the geometric median

annotations.note: Neither concept is defined, so this coincidence is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Centerpoint

anchor.snippet: a centerpoint of the set is a point such that any hyperplane that goes through that point divides the set of points in two roughly equal subsets

annotations.note: The centerpoint (Tukey depth) concept is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of a centerpoint

anchor.snippet: Every non-empty set of points (with no duplicates) has at least one centerpoint

annotations.note: The centerpoint theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional median

anchor.snippet: The conditional median occurs in the setting where we seek to estimate a random variable

annotations.note: Mathlib has conditional expectation but no conditional median.

annotations.label: Conditional median is optimal Bayesian estimator

anchor.snippet: The conditional median is the optimal Bayesian

annotations.note: Bayesian optimality of the conditional median under L1 loss is not in Mathlib.

annotations.note: This characterization of when the optimal estimator is linear is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: the interpolated median can be calculated from

annotations.note: The interpolated median is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pseudo-median

anchor.snippet: the population pseudo-median , which is the median of a symmetrized distribution

annotations.note: The pseudo-median is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Theil–Sen estimator

anchor.snippet: The Theil–Sen estimator is a method for robust linear regression based on finding medians of slopes

annotations.note: The Theil–Sen estimator is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median filter

anchor.snippet: The median filter is an important tool of image processing

annotations.note: The median filter (image processing) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: k-medians clustering

anchor.snippet: the k-medians clustering algorithm provides a way of defining clusters

annotations.note: The k-medians clustering algorithm is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median–median line

anchor.snippet: This is a method of robust regression

annotations.note: The median–median line regression method is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Median-unbiased estimator

anchor.snippet: will be said to be median-unbiased

annotations.note: Median-unbiasedness of estimators is not defined in Mathlib (no median, no estimator framework).

annotations.label: Mean-unbiased minimizes squared-error risk

anchor.snippet: Any mean -unbiased estimator minimizes the risk

annotations.note: Mathlib has variance lemmas (e.g. `variance_le_expectation_sq`) but no decision-theoretic statement that mean-unbiased estimators minimize squared-error risk.

annotations.label: Median-unbiased minimizes absolute-deviation risk

anchor.snippet: A median -unbiased estimator minimizes the risk with respect to the absolute-deviation loss function

annotations.note: The absolute-deviation risk optimality of median-unbiased estimators is not formalized in Mathlib.
